description: Trigger discrimination for security-event DETECTION/ALERTING design (this skill) vs the audit RECORD (audit-log-architect), telemetry/alert-config implementation (observability-operator), reliability SLOs/paging (slo-reliability-architect), and the post-alert playbook (incident-response-runbook).

cases.prompt: What security events should we be logging and alerting on, and who should each alert go to? I want to know we'd notice an attack.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — detection coverage + alerting rules + response wiring for SECURITY events is this skill.
cases.reason: Designing what security events to detect, what alerts fire, and where they route is this skill's core scope — not the record, not the implementation, not reliability paging.

cases.prompt: Design the audit log: event taxonomy, append-only record schema, tamper evidence, retention, and tenant-scoped access to it.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — the durable RECORD is audit-log-architect; it records but never detects or alerts.
cases.reason: audit-log-architect owns the record's taxonomy/schema/integrity/retention. This skill consumes those records to design detection and alerting on top.

cases.prompt: Here's the approved alerting spec — create the alert rules and dashboards in the monitoring stack and verify they fire.
cases.reason: Editing live alert/dashboard config and verifying telemetry is observability-operator (manual-only). This skill produces the spec being implemented and changes nothing itself.

cases.prompt: Define our SLOs and error budgets and decide which reliability symptoms should page versus ticket.
cases.reason: SLOs/SLIs and error-budget paging are reliability alerting on user-visible symptoms. This skill's alerts fire on attacker behavior, which may be invisible to users — different signal, different design.

cases.prompt: Write the runbook for what on-call does when the credential-stuffing alert fires — triage, containment, comms, postmortem.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — this skill designs what FIRES the alert and links each alert to a runbook; authoring the runbook itself is incident-response-runbook.
cases.reason: The reactive procedure AFTER an alert is incident-response-runbook. This skill is the upstream detection/alerting design that fires it.
